title: Is Now The Time To Reassess RLX Technology (RLX) After A 5‑Year 88% Share Price Slide
content:
Find winning stocks in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.

If you are wondering whether RLX Technology at around US$2.25 is a bargain or a value trap, you are not alone. The stock has been flat over the last week, with a 7.8% decline over 30 days, a 3.0% decline year to date, a 4.2% gain over 1 year, a 3.9% decline over 3 years, and an 87.8% decline over 5 years. These moves can change how investors think about both upside and risk. Recent coverage has focused on RLX Technology as a US listed Chinese e vapor company. Commentary has highlighted ongoing investor attention on regulation and long term industry prospects. This backdrop helps frame why the share price has been relatively steady in the near term compared with its much sharper moves over longer periods. On our framework, RLX Technology scores 4 out of 6 on valuation checks. We will unpack this using several common approaches first, before finishing with a more complete way of looking at what the market might be pricing in.

Find out why RLX Technology's 4.2% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Approach 1: RLX Technology Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model projects a company’s future cash flows and then discounts them back to today’s value to estimate what the whole business might be worth right now.

For RLX Technology, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is reported at CN¥1,246.5m, and Simply Wall St extends analyst style estimates to create a ten year path. For example, projected free cash flow for 2026 is CN¥1,364.6m, moving to CN¥2,101.0m by 2035, with each year discounted back to today in the model.

Bringing those cash flows together, the DCF estimate points to an intrinsic value of US$3.86 per share for RLX Technology, compared with a current share price of about US$2.25. That gap implies the shares trade at a 41.7% discount to the model’s estimate of fair value.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests RLX Technology is undervalued by 41.7%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 48 more high quality undervalued stocks.RLX Discounted Cash Flow as at Mar 2026

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for RLX Technology.

Approach 2: RLX Technology Price vs Earnings

For a business that is generating profits, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to see what investors are currently willing to pay for each dollar of earnings. It effectively bundles together expectations about future growth and the risk investors see in those earnings.

Story Continues

In general, higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually call for a lower, more cautious multiple. So the question is not whether a P/E is high or low in isolation, but whether it makes sense for this specific company.

RLX Technology currently trades on a P/E of 24.7x, compared with the Tobacco industry average of 12.9x and a peer average of 33.0x. Simply Wall St also uses a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for the P/E, which estimates what a reasonable multiple could be after taking into account factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks. This tailored Fair Ratio is designed to be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry, because it adjusts for the company’s own profile. On this framework, RLX Technology’s current P/E suggests the shares are undervalued relative to that Fair Ratio.

Result: UNDERVALUEDNYSE:RLX P/E Ratio as at Mar 2026

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 18 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your RLX Technology Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce Narratives. This is a simple way for you to write the story you believe about RLX Technology, link that story to specific forecasts for revenue, earnings and margins, and then see the fair value that falls out of your assumptions. All of this is available inside the Narratives tool on Simply Wall St's Community page, where you can compare that fair value with the current share price to help decide whether RLX looks interesting for you now or later. You can watch that view update as new news or earnings arrive, and see how different investors can look at the same company and reach very different conclusions. For example, one Narrative anchors on a lower fair value around US$2.34 because it focuses on tighter regulation and concentration in China, and another points to a higher fair value near US$4.02 because it places more weight on international expansion, multi category nicotine products and buybacks.
